title: Ateleia pterocarpa
taxonomy: Ateleia pterocarpa is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It is a member of the subfamily Faboideae and the tribe Loteae. The plant is native to the tropical and subtropical regions of Asia. Further classification of Ateleia pterocarpa includes its genus Ateleia, which belongs to the family Fabaceae. The exact classification of Ateleia pterocarpa can be summarized as a member of the family Fabaceae, genus Ateleia, and the tribe Loteae.
uses: Ateleia pterocarpa is a plant species used in traditional medicine in certain regions. Its roots and leaves are used to treat various health conditions, including fever and digestive problems. In traditional medicine, it is also used to treat skin conditions and wounds. The plant's antibacterial properties are believed to contribute to its medicinal effects. Further research is needed to confirm the plant's efficacy and potential side effects.
